title: Introduction to Dormancy: Detecting Hidden Patterns in Data
text: dormancy package introduces novel framework detecting analyzing dormant patterns multivariate data. Unlike traditional pattern detection methods focus currently active relationships, dormancy identifies statistical patterns exist remain inactive specific trigger conditions emerge.

title: Introduction to Dormancy: Detecting Hidden Patterns in Data
text: dormant pattern genuine statistical relationship currently suppressed prevailing conditions data. Consider real-world analogies: Biological dormancy: Seeds can remain dormant years, germinating conditions (temperature, moisture) right Geological dormancy: Fault lines can dormant centuries triggering earthquakes Epidemiological latency: Pathogens can exist latent state becoming active data analysis, dormant patterns manifest : Relationships strong specific data regions weak overall Correlations emerge certain threshold conditions met Patterns hidden confounding variables , controlled , reveal strong associations

title: Introduction to Dormancy: Detecting Hidden Patterns in Data
text: Traditional correlation analysis can miss dormant patterns : Aggregate statistics mask conditional relationships Weak overall correlations may hide strong local correlations Threshold effects create piecewise relationships Phase-dependent patterns vary across data space dormancy package addresses limitations specialized detection methods.

title: Introduction to Dormancy: Detecting Hidden Patterns in Data
code: library(dormancy)

title: Introduction to Dormancy: Detecting Hidden Patterns in Data
text: Let’s create simple example dormant pattern: overall correlation masks strong relationship exists specific conditions. Let’s detect dormant pattern: detection reveals relationship x y dormant - becomes active condition == 1.
code: set.seed(42) n <- 500  # Create variables x <- rnorm(n) condition <- sample(c(0, 1), n, replace = TRUE)  # Relationship between x and y only exists when condition == 1 y <- ifelse(condition == 1,              0.8 * x + rnorm(n, 0, 0.3),  # Strong relationship             rnorm(n))                      # No relationship  data <- data.frame(x = x, y = y, condition = factor(condition))  # Overall correlation is weak cor(data$x, data$y) #> [1] 0.4575864 result <- dormancy_detect(data, method = "conditional", verbose = TRUE) #> Starting dormant pattern detection using method: conditional #> Analyzing 1 variable pairs... #> Detection complete. Found 0 dormant patterns. print(result) #> Dormancy Detection Results #> ========================== #>  #> Method: conditional  #> Threshold: 0.3  #> Observations: 500  #> Variables: 2  #>  #> Patterns detected: 0

title: Introduction to Dormancy: Detecting Hidden Patterns in Data
text: package provides four complementary detection methods:

title: Introduction to Dormancy: Detecting Hidden Patterns in Data
text: Identifies patterns conditionally suppressed - active specific conditions. method works segmenting data space comparing relationship strengths across segments.
code: result_cond <- dormancy_detect(data, method = "conditional")

title: Introduction to Dormancy: Detecting Hidden Patterns in Data
text: Identifies patterns emerge variables cross specific thresholds.
code: set.seed(123) n <- 400 x <- runif(n, -2, 2)  # Relationship only emerges for extreme values of x y <- ifelse(abs(x) > 1,             0.9 * sign(x) + rnorm(n, 0, 0.2),             rnorm(n, 0, 0.5))  data_thresh <- data.frame(x = x, y = y)  result_thresh <- dormancy_detect(data_thresh, method = "threshold") print(result_thresh) #> Dormancy Detection Results #> ========================== #>  #> Method: threshold  #> Threshold: 0.3  #> Observations: 400  #> Variables: 2  #>  #> Patterns detected: 0

title: Introduction to Dormancy: Detecting Hidden Patterns in Data
text: Identifies patterns exist specific phase regions data space.
code: set.seed(456) n <- 300 t <- seq(0, 4*pi, length.out = n) x <- sin(t) + rnorm(n, 0, 0.2) y <- cos(t) + rnorm(n, 0, 0.2)  # Add relationship only in certain phases phase <- atan2(y, x) y <- ifelse(phase > 0 & phase < pi/2,             0.7 * x + rnorm(n, 0, 0.2),             y)  data_phase <- data.frame(x = x, y = y)  result_phase <- dormancy_detect(data_phase, method = "phase")

title: Introduction to Dormancy: Detecting Hidden Patterns in Data
text: Identifies patterns trigger chain reactions variables.
code: set.seed(789) n <- 300 x <- rnorm(n) y <- rnorm(n) z <- rnorm(n)  # Relationship x-y emerges when z is extreme y <- ifelse(abs(z) > 1.5,             0.8 * x + rnorm(n, 0, 0.2),             y)  data_cascade <- data.frame(x = x, y = y, z = z)  result_cascade <- dormancy_detect(data_cascade, method = "cascade")

title: Introduction to Dormancy: Detecting Hidden Patterns in Data
text: dormant patterns detected, analyze trigger conditions: trigger analysis provides: - Specific trigger conditions pattern - Confidence intervals via bootstrap - Activation probabilities - Actionable recommendations
code: set.seed(42) n <- 500 x <- rnorm(n) z <- sample(c(0, 1), n, replace = TRUE) y <- ifelse(z == 1, 0.8 * x + rnorm(n, 0, 0.3), rnorm(n)) data <- data.frame(x = x, y = y, z = factor(z))  result <- dormancy_detect(data, method = "conditional")  if (nrow(result$patterns) > 0) {   triggers <- dormancy_trigger(result, sensitivity = 0.5, n_bootstrap = 50)   print(triggers) }

title: Introduction to Dormancy: Detecting Hidden Patterns in Data
text: Measure “deeply asleep” dormant pattern : Depth measurements help prioritize monitoring: - Shallow dormancy: Easily activated, needs immediate attention - Deep dormancy: Requires significant changes activate, lower priority
code: if (nrow(result$patterns) > 0) {   depths <- dormancy_depth(result, method = "combined")   print(depths) }

title: Introduction to Dormancy: Detecting Hidden Patterns in Data
text: Evaluate risk associated dormant patterns: Risk assessment considers: - Activation probability - Impact magnitude - Cascade potential - Uncertainty estimates
code: if (nrow(result$patterns) > 0) {   risk <- dormancy_risk(result, time_horizon = 1, risk_tolerance = 0.3)   print(risk) }

title: Introduction to Dormancy: Detecting Hidden Patterns in Data
text: Simulate happen dormant pattern activated: valuable : - Scenario planning - Stress testing - Understanding potential system behaviors
code: if (nrow(result$patterns) > 0) {   awakening <- awaken(result, pattern_id = 1, intensity = 1, n_sim = 100)   print(awakening) }

title: Introduction to Dormancy: Detecting Hidden Patterns in Data
text: Identify patterns active become dormant:
code: set.seed(42) n <- 400 time <- 1:n x <- rnorm(n)  # Relationship that fades over time effect_strength <- exp(-time / 150) y <- effect_strength * 0.8 * x + (1 - effect_strength) * rnorm(n)  data_time <- data.frame(time = time, x = x, y = y)  hib <- hibernate(data_time, time_var = "time", window_size = 0.15) print(hib) #> Pattern Hibernation Analysis #> ============================ #>  #> Window size: 60  #> Hibernation threshold: 0.3  #>  #> Hibernated Patterns: #>     variable_1 variable_2 early_correlation late_correlation correlation_change #> x~y          x          y         0.9398223         0.175471          0.7643512 #>     hibernation_time hibernation_depth #> x~y            133.5         0.7643512 #>  #> Revival Potential: #>   variable_pair revival_potential revival_difficulty #> 1           x~y         0.2356488          Difficult

title: Introduction to Dormancy: Detecting Hidden Patterns in Data
text: Map data space find regions dormant patterns might emerge:
code: set.seed(42) n <- 300 x <- rnorm(n) y <- rnorm(n)  # Create a region with hidden pattern mask <- x > 1 & y > 1 y[mask] <- 0.9 * x[mask] + rnorm(sum(mask), 0, 0.1)  data_scout <- data.frame(x = x, y = y)  scout <- dormancy_scout(data_scout, grid_resolution = 15, scout_method = "density") print(scout) #> Dormancy Scout Results #> ====================== #>  #> Method: density  #> Grid resolution: 15  #>  #> Variable Pair Analysis: #>     variable_pair max_potential mean_potential n_hotspots #> x~y           x~y     0.9833333      0.7008741         20 #>  #> Top Dormancy Hotspots: #>       x_coord     y_coord potential variable_pair #> 2   0.2611848 -2.46133548 0.9833333           x~y #> 5   1.0747535 -2.05487798 0.9833333           x~y #> 6   1.4815379 -2.05487798 0.9833333           x~y #> 9  -2.1795214 -0.83550548 0.9833333           x~y #> 13 -2.5863057 -0.02259049 0.9833333           x~y #> 16  2.2951066  0.38386701 0.9833333           x~y #> 17 -1.7727370  1.19678201 0.9833333           x~y #> 19 -0.9591683  2.00969700 0.9833333           x~y #> 20  0.2611848  2.41615450 0.9833333           x~y #> 1  -0.1455995 -2.46133548 0.9800000           x~y

title: Introduction to Dormancy: Detecting Hidden Patterns in Data
text: Plot results:
code: # Overview plot dormancy:::plot.dormancy(result, type = "overview")  # Pattern network dormancy:::plot.dormancy(result, type = "patterns")  # Risk-focused dormancy:::plot.dormancy(result, type = "risk")  # Scout map dormancy:::plot.dormancy_map(scout) dormancy:::plot.dormancy_map(scout)

code: # Assume financial_data has returns for multiple assets result <- dormancy_detect(financial_data, method = "threshold") risk <- dormancy_risk(result, time_horizon = 30)  # 30-day horizon

title: Introduction to Dormancy: Detecting Hidden Patterns in Data
text: Find patterns emerge certain production conditions:
code: # Assume production_data with process variables result <- dormancy_detect(production_data, method = "conditional") triggers <- dormancy_trigger(result)

code: # Assume environmental_data with sensor readings scout <- dormancy_scout(environmental_data) hib <- hibernate(environmental_data, time_var = "date")

title: Introduction to Dormancy: Detecting Hidden Patterns in Data
text: dormancy package provides comprehensive toolkit : Detection: Four methods finding dormant patterns Characterization: Understand trigger conditions dormancy depth Risk Assessment: Quantify activation risk potential impact Simulation: Explore “-” scenarios Monitoring: Track patterns hibernated scout future risks focusing patterns become active rather just currently active patterns, gain insights latent risks opportunities traditional methods miss.

title: Detection and Analysis of Dormant Patterns in Data
text: first statistical package dedicated dormant pattern detection. concept inspired : Biological dormancy: Seeds remaining dormant conditions right Geological phenomena: Dormant faults can trigger earthquakes Epidemiology: Latent infections activate stress data analysis, dormant patterns relationships : strong specific data regions weak overall emerge certain thresholds crossed masked confounding variables trigger cascade effects activated

title: Detection and Analysis of Dormant Patterns in Data
code: # Install from CRAN (when available) install.packages("dormancy")  # Install development version # devtools::install_github("danymukesha/dormancy")

title: Detection and Analysis of Dormant Patterns in Data
code: library(dormancy)  set.seed(42) n <- 500  # Create data with a dormant pattern x <- rnorm(n) condition <- sample(c(0, 1), n, replace = TRUE)  # Relationship only exists when condition == 1 y <- ifelse(condition == 1,              0.8 * x + rnorm(n, 0, 0.3),              rnorm(n))  data <- data.frame(x = x, y = y, condition = factor(condition))  # Overall correlation is weak cor(data$x, data$y)  # ~0.35  # Detect the dormant pattern result <- dormancy_detect(data, method = "conditional") print(result) #> Dormant pattern detected: x ~ y #> Dormancy score: 0.72 #> Trigger: condition == 1

what: Simulate Awakening of Dormant Patterns — awaken
title: Simulate Awakening of Dormant Patterns — awaken
text: Simulates happen dormant pattern "awaken" - .e., become active. function allows exploration potential future states scenario analysis without waiting actual pattern activation.

title: Simulate Awakening of Dormant Patterns — awaken
code: awaken(   dormancy_result,   pattern_id = 1,   intensity = 1,   n_sim = 100,   return_data = FALSE,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Simulate Awakening of Dormant Patterns — awaken
text: dormancy_result object class "dormancy" dormancy_detect. pattern_id Integer "". pattern(s) awaken. Default 1. intensity Numeric. Intensity awakening, 0 (dormant) 1 (fully active). Default 1. n_sim Integer. Number simulation runs. Default 100. return_data Logical. Whether return simulated data. Default FALSE. verbose Logical. Whether print progress messages. Default FALSE.

title: Simulate Awakening of Dormant Patterns — awaken
text: list containing: awakening_effects - Data frame describing effects awakening simulated_stats - Summary statistics simulations cascade_effects - Effects patterns () simulated_data - return_data = TRUE, simulated datasets

title: Simulate Awakening of Dormant Patterns — awaken
text: Awakening simulation valuable : Scenario planning stress testing Understanding potential system behaviors Preparing pattern activation events Testing robustness current strategies simulation works : Identifying dormant pattern's trigger conditions Simulating data conditions met Applying pattern's relationship simulated data Measuring resulting effects system

title: Simulate Awakening of Dormant Patterns — awaken
code: set.seed(42) n <- 500 x <- rnorm(n) z <- sample(c(0, 1), n, replace = TRUE) y <- ifelse(z == 1, 0.8 * x + rnorm(sum(z), 0, 0.3), rnorm(n)) #> Warning: longer object length is not a multiple of shorter object length data <- data.frame(x = x, y = y, z = factor(z))  result <- dormancy_detect(data, method = "conditional") awakening <- awaken(result, pattern_id = 1, n_sim = 50) #> Warning: No dormant patterns to awaken. print(awakening) #> $awakening_effects #> data frame with 0 columns and 0 rows #>  #> $simulated_stats #> NULL #>  #> $cascade_effects #> NULL #>

what: dormancy: Detection and Analysis of Dormant Patterns in Data — dormancy-package
title: dormancy: Detection and Analysis of Dormant Patterns in Data — dormancy-package
text: novel framework detecting, quantifying, analyzing dormant patterns multivariate data. Dormant patterns statistical relationships exist data remain inactive specific trigger conditions emerge. concept, inspired biological dormancy (seeds, pathogens) geological phenomena (dormant faults), provides tools identify latent risks, hidden correlations, potential phase transitions complex systems. package introduces methods quantifying dormancy depth, trigger sensitivity, awakening risk - enabling analysts discover patterns conventional methods miss focus currently active relationships.

title: dormancy: Detection and Analysis of Dormant Patterns in Data — dormancy-package
text: Maintainer: Dany Mukesha danymukesha@gmail.com (ORCID)

what: Measure the Depth of Dormancy in Patterns — dormancy_depth
title: Measure the Depth of Dormancy in Patterns — dormancy_depth
text: Quantifies "deeply asleep" dormant pattern - measuring energy required activate stability dormant state. Deeper dormancy implies greater resistance activation potentially larger effects awakened.

title: Measure the Depth of Dormancy in Patterns — dormancy_depth
code: dormancy_depth(   dormancy_result,   method = "combined",   normalize = TRUE,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Measure the Depth of Dormancy in Patterns — dormancy_depth
text: dormancy_result object class "dormancy" dormancy_detect. method Character. depth measurement method: "energy" - Measures statistical "energy barrier" activation "stability" - Measures stable dormant state "entropy" - Measures information-theoretic dormancy depth "combined" - Weighted combination methods Default "combined". normalize Logical. Whether normalize depth scores [0, 1]. Default TRUE. verbose Logical. Whether print progress messages. Default FALSE.

title: Measure the Depth of Dormancy in Patterns — dormancy_depth
text: list containing: depths - Data frame depth measurements pattern depth_distribution - Summary statistics depth distribution awakening_effort - Estimated effort required activate pattern stability_index - Stability measure pattern's dormant state

title: Measure the Depth of Dormancy in Patterns — dormancy_depth
text: Dormancy depth novel concept statistical analysis, inspired : Physics: Potential energy barriers phase transitions Biology: Depth seed dormancy (stratification requirements) Geology: Locked fault segments earthquake potential deeply dormant pattern: Requires significant change conditions activate stable minor perturbations May larger effect finally awakened Represents accumulated "potential energy" system depth measurement helps prioritize patterns monitor magnitude change required awaken .

title: Measure the Depth of Dormancy in Patterns — dormancy_depth
code: set.seed(42) n <- 500 x <- rnorm(n) # Create a deeply dormant pattern (only active in extreme conditions) z <- ifelse(abs(x) > 2, 1, 0) y <- ifelse(z == 1, 0.9 * x + rnorm(sum(z), 0, 0.1), rnorm(n)) #> Warning: longer object length is not a multiple of shorter object length data <- data.frame(x = x, y = y)  result <- dormancy_detect(data, method = "threshold") depths <- dormancy_depth(result) #> Warning: No dormant patterns to measure depth for. print(depths) #> $depths #> data frame with 0 columns and 0 rows #>  #> $depth_distribution #> NULL #>  #> $awakening_effort #> NULL #>  #> $stability_index #> NULL #>

what: Detect Dormant Patterns in Multivariate Data — dormancy_detect
title: Detect Dormant Patterns in Multivariate Data — dormancy_detect
text: Identifies dormant patterns multivariate data - statistical relationships exist currently inactive. Dormant patterns manifest specific trigger conditions emerge data.

title: Detect Dormant Patterns in Multivariate Data — dormancy_detect
code: dormancy_detect(   data,   threshold = 0.3,   method = "conditional",   n_bins = 10,   min_cluster = 0.05,   parallel = FALSE,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Detect Dormant Patterns in Multivariate Data — dormancy_detect
text: data numeric matrix data frame observations rows variables columns. threshold Numeric. minimum dormancy score pattern considered significant. Default 0.3. method Character. detection method use. Options : "conditional" - Detects patterns conditionally suppressed     (active specific conditions) "threshold" - Detects patterns emerge variables cross     certain thresholds "phase" - Detects patterns exist specific phase regions     data space "cascade" - Detects cascade-ready patterns trigger     chain reactions Default "conditional". n_bins Integer. Number bins discretizing continuous variables searching conditional patterns. Default 10. min_cluster Numeric. Minimum proportion observations must region considered. Default 0.05 (5%). parallel Logical. Whether use parallel processing. Default FALSE. verbose Logical. Whether print progress messages. Default FALSE.

title: Detect Dormant Patterns in Multivariate Data — dormancy_detect
text: list class "dormancy" containing: patterns - data frame detected dormant patterns columns:     variables, dormancy_score, trigger_variable, trigger_region, activation_risk data - input data method - detection method used threshold - threshold used conditional_regions - List regions patterns dormant metadata - Additional information detection process

title: Detect Dormant Patterns in Multivariate Data — dormancy_detect
text: Dormant patterns fundamentally different weak spurious correlations. dormant pattern genuine relationship currently suppressed prevailing conditions data. key insight : pattern exists (verified subset analysis) pattern currently inactive (visible aggregate statistics) pattern can "awaken" conditions change detection algorithm works : Segmenting data space regions Computing pairwise relationships region Identifying relationships vary significantly across regions Flagging relationships strong regions weak overall Estimating conditions dormant patterns activate

title: Detect Dormant Patterns in Multivariate Data — dormancy_detect
code: # Create data with a dormant pattern set.seed(42) n <- 1000 x <- rnorm(n) z <- sample(c(0, 1), n, replace = TRUE) # Relationship between x and y only exists when z == 1 y <- ifelse(z == 1, 0.8 * x + rnorm(n, 0, 0.3), rnorm(n)) data <- data.frame(x = x, y = y, z = factor(z))  # Detect dormant patterns result <- dormancy_detect(data, method = "conditional") print(result) #> Dormancy Detection Results #> ========================== #>  #> Method: conditional  #> Threshold: 0.3  #> Observations: 1000  #> Variables: 2  #>  #> Patterns detected: 0

what: Assess Risk of Dormant Pattern Activation — dormancy_risk
title: Assess Risk of Dormant Pattern Activation — dormancy_risk
text: Quantifies risk associated dormant patterns, including probability activation, potential impact, uncertainty risk estimates. function provides actionable risk metrics decision-making monitoring priorities.

title: Assess Risk of Dormant Pattern Activation — dormancy_risk
code: dormancy_risk(   dormancy_result,   depth_result = NULL,   impact_weights = NULL,   time_horizon = 1,   risk_tolerance = 0.3,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Assess Risk of Dormant Pattern Activation — dormancy_risk
text: dormancy_result object class "dormancy" dormancy_detect. depth_result Optional. object class "dormancy_depth" dormancy_depth. provided, uses depth information accurate risk assessment. impact_weights Optional named vector weights different impact types. Default considers symmetric positive/negative impacts. time_horizon Numeric. time horizon risk assessment (abstract units). Longer horizons increase activation probability. Default 1. risk_tolerance Numeric. Risk tolerance threshold flagging. Default 0.3. verbose Logical. Whether print progress messages. Default FALSE.

title: Assess Risk of Dormant Pattern Activation — dormancy_risk
text: list containing: risk_scores - Data frame risk metrics pattern risk_matrix - Matrix activation probability x impact priorities - Ordered list patterns risk priority recommendations - Risk management recommendations summary - Overall risk summary statistics

title: Assess Risk of Dormant Pattern Activation — dormancy_risk
text: Risk assessment dormant patterns considers multiple dimensions: Activation Probability: Likelihood trigger conditions     met given time horizon Impact Magnitude: Expected effect size pattern activates Impact Direction: Whether activation beneficial,     harmful, neutral Cascade Potential: Risk triggering patterns Uncertainty: Confidence risk estimates risk score combines dimensions actionable metric: $$Risk = P(activation) \times Impact \times CascadeFactor \times (1 + Uncertainty)$$

title: Assess Risk of Dormant Pattern Activation — dormancy_risk
code: set.seed(42) n <- 500 x <- rnorm(n) z <- sample(c(0, 1), n, replace = TRUE) y <- ifelse(z == 1, 0.8 * x + rnorm(sum(z), 0, 0.3), rnorm(n)) #> Warning: longer object length is not a multiple of shorter object length data <- data.frame(x = x, y = y, z = factor(z))  result <- dormancy_detect(data, method = "conditional") risk <- dormancy_risk(result, time_horizon = 2) #> Warning: No dormant patterns to assess risk for. print(risk) #> $risk_scores #> data frame with 0 columns and 0 rows #>  #> $risk_matrix #> NULL #>  #> $priorities #> character(0) #>  #> $recommendations #> [1] "No dormant patterns detected." #>  #> $summary #> NULL #>

what: Scout for Dormant Pattern Regions in Data — dormancy_scout
title: Scout for Dormant Pattern Regions in Data — dormancy_scout
text: Systematically scans data space identify regions dormant patterns might emerge. Unlike dormancy_detect identifies specific patterns, dormancy_scout maps "terrain" dormancy potential.

title: Scout for Dormant Pattern Regions in Data — dormancy_scout
code: dormancy_scout(   data,   grid_resolution = 20,   scout_method = "density",   return_map = TRUE,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Scout for Dormant Pattern Regions in Data — dormancy_scout
text: data numeric matrix data frame. grid_resolution Integer. Resolution scanning grid. Higher values give finer resolution slower computation. Default 20. scout_method Character. Scanning method: "density" - Identifies low-density regions patterns might hide "variance" - Identifies high-variance regions pattern potential "correlation" - Maps local correlation landscapes "entropy" - Identifies high-entropy regions dormancy potential Default "density". return_map Logical. Whether return full dormancy map. Default TRUE. verbose Logical. Whether print progress messages. Default FALSE.

title: Scout for Dormant Pattern Regions in Data — dormancy_scout
text: list containing: scout_results - Data frame coordinates dormancy potential hotspots - Regions highest dormancy potential dormancy_map - return_map = TRUE, matrix representing     dormancy landscape summary - Summary statistics scan

title: Scout for Dormant Pattern Regions in Data — dormancy_scout
text: Scout analysis useful : Identifying regions monitor future pattern emergence Understanding "geography" data's pattern space Finding data regions underexplored anomalous Planning targeted data collection high-potential regions scout creates map "dormancy potential" - actual patterns, locations patterns likely exist emerge.

title: Scout for Dormant Pattern Regions in Data — dormancy_scout
code: set.seed(42) n <- 500 x <- rnorm(n) y <- rnorm(n) # Create a region with hidden pattern z <- ifelse(x > 1 & y > 1, 0.9 * x + rnorm(sum(x > 1 & y > 1), 0, 0.1), y) #> Warning: longer object length is not a multiple of shorter object length data <- data.frame(x = x, y = z)  scout <- dormancy_scout(data, grid_resolution = 15) print(scout) #> Dormancy Scout Results #> ====================== #>  #> Method: density  #> Grid resolution: 15  #>  #> Variable Pair Analysis: #>     variable_pair max_potential mean_potential n_hotspots #> x~y           x~y          0.99      0.7106933         21 #>  #> Top Dormancy Hotspots: #>      x_coord    y_coord potential variable_pair #> 3  -2.141811 -1.4097267      0.99           x~y #> 4   2.540226 -0.9192236      0.99           x~y #> 5  -2.567450 -0.4287205      0.99           x~y #> 9  -2.567450  0.5522857      0.99           x~y #> 10  2.540226  0.5522857      0.99           x~y #> 11 -2.567450  1.0427888      0.99           x~y #> 14 -2.141811  1.5332919      0.99           x~y #> 17  1.688946  2.0237950      0.99           x~y #> 18 -0.439252  2.5142981      0.99           x~y #> 21  0.837667  2.5142981      0.99           x~y

what: Identify Trigger Conditions for Dormant Patterns — dormancy_trigger
title: Identify Trigger Conditions for Dormant Patterns — dormancy_trigger
text: Analyzes detected dormant patterns identify specific conditions activate. function goes beyond detection characterize precise trigger mechanisms sensitivity.

title: Identify Trigger Conditions for Dormant Patterns — dormancy_trigger
code: dormancy_trigger(   dormancy_result,   sensitivity = 0.5,   n_bootstrap = 100,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Identify Trigger Conditions for Dormant Patterns — dormancy_trigger
text: dormancy_result object class "dormancy" dormancy_detect. sensitivity Numeric. sensitivity level trigger detection, ranging 0 (low sensitivity, major triggers) 1 (high sensitivity, minor triggers included). Default 0.5. n_bootstrap Integer. Number bootstrap samples confidence intervals. Default 100. verbose Logical. Whether print progress messages. Default FALSE.

title: Identify Trigger Conditions for Dormant Patterns — dormancy_trigger
text: list containing: triggers - data frame trigger details:     pattern_id, trigger_variable, trigger_type, threshold_value,     sensitivity_score, confidence_lower, confidence_upper trigger_map - matrix showing trigger relationships recommendations - Character vector actionable insights

title: Identify Trigger Conditions for Dormant Patterns — dormancy_trigger
text: Trigger identification crucial risk management early warning systems. dormant pattern might triggered : Threshold triggers: variable crosses specific value Region triggers: observations fall within specific data regions Categorical triggers: categorical condition met Compound triggers: multiple conditions align Temporal triggers: time-dependent patterns emerge function uses bootstrap resampling provide confidence intervals around trigger estimates, ensuring robust identification even limited data.

title: Identify Trigger Conditions for Dormant Patterns — dormancy_trigger
code: set.seed(42) n <- 500 x <- rnorm(n) z <- sample(c(0, 1), n, replace = TRUE) y <- ifelse(z == 1, 0.8 * x + rnorm(n, 0, 0.3), rnorm(n)) data <- data.frame(x = x, y = y, z = factor(z))  result <- dormancy_detect(data, method = "conditional") triggers <- dormancy_trigger(result) #> Warning: No dormant patterns detected. Run dormancy_detect first. print(triggers) #> $triggers #> data frame with 0 columns and 0 rows #>  #> $trigger_map #>      [,1] #> [1,]   NA #>  #> $recommendations #> [1] "No dormant patterns to analyze." #>

what: Identify Patterns That Have Become Dormant Over Time — hibernate
title: Identify Patterns That Have Become Dormant Over Time — hibernate
text: Analyzes time series sequential data identify patterns active become dormant. inverse problem dormancy_detect - finding patterns "went sleep" rather patterns currently dormant.

title: Identify Patterns That Have Become Dormant Over Time — hibernate
code: hibernate(   data,   time_var = NULL,   window_size = 0.2,   threshold = 0.3,   min_observations = 30,   verbose = FALSE )

title: Identify Patterns That Have Become Dormant Over Time — hibernate
text: data data frame time column sequential index. time_var Character. Name time/sequence variable. NULL, uses row order sequence. Default NULL. window_size Integer numeric. Size rolling window detecting changes. integer, uses number observations. numeric < 1, uses proportion data. Default 0.2 (20% data). threshold Numeric. Minimum change pattern strength considered hibernation. Default 0.3. min_observations Integer. Minimum observations required analysis. Default 30. verbose Logical. Whether print progress messages. Default FALSE.

title: Identify Patterns That Have Become Dormant Over Time — hibernate
text: list containing: hibernated_patterns - Patterns become dormant timeline - patterns transitioned dormancy hibernation_depth - deeply patterns hibernated revival_potential - Likelihood patterns reawaken

title: Identify Patterns That Have Become Dormant Over Time — hibernate
text: Hibernation detection important : Understanding system evolution regime changes Identifying lost relationships might return Detecting structural breaks relationships Monitoring degradation system components pattern considered "hibernated" : strong earlier time window weakened significantly recent windows weakening due noise reduced sample size

title: Identify Patterns That Have Become Dormant Over Time — hibernate
code: set.seed(42) n <- 500 time <- 1:n x <- rnorm(n) # Relationship that fades over time effect_strength <- exp(-time / 200) y <- effect_strength * 0.8 * x + (1 - effect_strength) * rnorm(n) data <- data.frame(time = time, x = x, y = y)  hib <- hibernate(data, time_var = "time", window_size = 0.15) print(hib) #> Pattern Hibernation Analysis #> ============================ #>  #> Window size: 75  #> Hibernation threshold: 0.3  #>  #> Hibernated Patterns: #>     variable_1 variable_2 early_correlation late_correlation correlation_change #> x~y          x          y         0.9574825      -0.05163983          0.9058426 #>     hibernation_time hibernation_depth #> x~y              258         0.9058426 #>  #> Revival Potential: #>   variable_pair revival_potential revival_difficulty #> 1           x~y        0.09415736          Difficult
